summary: Jeff Atwood responds to the 'Five Things You Didn't Know About Me' meme by offering a detailed tour of his office at Vertigo Software, revealing his personality through his workspace and personal anecdotes. He initially resists the meme, citing Kathy Sierra's advice that 'nobody cares' about speaker backgrounds, but comes to realize that blogs are as much about the writer as the content. The post showcases his carefully curated office filled with quirky items like a Pong clock, thinking putty, and vintage software boxes. He concludes with five personal revelations including being arrested for phreaking in 1987, being terrible at math despite loving computers, and meeting his wife while dancing incorrectly to The Safety Dance.
key_points: Blogs are as much about the writer's personality as their stated topic - avoiding personal content is just as much a mistake as over-sharing
A developer's workspace reflects their personality and values, from ergonomic equipment to quirky collectibles
Atwood's background includes being arrested for wardialing and phreaking as a teenager in 1987, highlighting the lengths early computer enthusiasts went to avoid long distance charges
Despite loving computers, Atwood struggles with math - scoring in the 99.9th percentile on verbal GRE but only 77th in math
Behind every fascinating blog is a fascinating person, and sharing personal details creates human connection with readers
money_quotes: Blogs, for better or worse, are as much about the writer as they are any other topic.
Personality is the essential ingredient that makes blogs so interesting, so compelling, so... human.
Behind every fascinating blog is a fascinating person.
I love computers, but I can't stand math. No matter how hard I apply myself, I'm terrible at it.
In the dark ages before the internet, all we had were BBS systems and modems. And long distance charges were incredibly expensive back then.
To get your BBS fix, you had to find some way around the long distance charges.
I turned around and there was this cute girl telling me 'you're doing it wrong.' As in, I was doing the Safety Dance wrong.
I do think I'd have sex with my computers if it was physically possible.
key_insight: A blogger's personality and personal story are not distractions from their content but essential ingredients that make their writing compelling and human.
